2605.26942.title: Neuro-Symbolic Verification of LLM Outputs for Data-Sensitive Domains (extended preprint)
2605.26942.paper_summary: LLMs deployed in high-stakes domains face fundamental reliability challenges: hallucinations, inconsistencies, and privacy vulnerabilities introduce unacceptable risks where errors carry legal, financial, or safety consequences. This paper presents a hybrid verification architecture combining formal symbolic methods with neural semantic analysis to provide complementary guarantees for LLM-generated content. This architecture employs logical reasoning for input verification, leveraging completeness properties to provide decidable guarantees on structured requirements. For output validation, embedding-based semantic similarity detects contextual hallucinations where formal methods lack expressiveness. This separation is realized in a parallel, actor-based pipeline, addressing limitations of prompt-based self-verification approaches, which inherit the distributional biases that produce hallucinations. The proposed architecture and type-aware verification method are validated with HAIMEDA, a real-world medical device damage assessment reporting system developed through Action Design Research. Evaluation shows hallucination detection rates of over 83% for structured entities and 72% for semantic fabrications, with a 30% reduction in report creation time, demonstrating that neuro-symbolic architectures can provide principled safeguards for LLM deployment in data-sensitive domains.
2605.26942.paper_summary_zh: LLMs 在高風險領域的部署面臨基本的可靠性挑戰：幻覺、不一致性和隱私漏洞引入了不可接受的風險，因為錯誤可能帶來法律、財務或安全後果。本文提出了一種混合驗證架構，結合了形式符號方法和神經語義分析，以提供對 LLM 生成內容的互補保證。該架構利用邏輯推理進行輸入驗證，利用完備性特性為結構化要求提供可決定的保證。對於輸出驗證，基於嵌入的語義相似性檢測上下文幻覺，這是形式方法缺乏表達能力的地方。這種分離在一個並行的基於演員的管道中實現，解決了基於提示的自我驗證方法的局限性，這些方法繼承了產生幻覺的分佈偏見。所提出的架構和類型感知驗證方法通過 HAIMEDA 進行了驗證，HAIMEDA 是一個通過行動設計研究開發的現實世界醫療設備損壞評估報告系統。評估顯示，對於結構化實體的幻覺檢測率超過 83%，對於語義虛構的檢測率為 72%，報告創建時間減少了 30%，證明神經符號架構可以為 LLM 在數據敏感領域的部署提供原則性保障。
2605.26942.author: Paul Sigloch et.al.
